habitat: Deciduous/mixed forests; urban areas; broadleaf/coniferous forest; and wooded parks
story: An icon of the skies, the Northen Boobook (Ninox japonica) is a species from the Strigidae family. It thrives in Deciduous/mixed forests; urban areas; broadleaf/coniferous forest; and wooded parks, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the Northen Boobook is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Northen Boobook helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Northen Boobook, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
